message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE A CHESS-RESOURCE RESEARCHER. Category: "Tactics & Calculation Training".

Scrodinger already has a strong tactical eye (puzzle 2073-2212) but needs SPEED and CALCULATION DEPTH (his blitz lags rapid by 500 pts). Find the best tactics & calculation resources: platforms (lichess puzzles, Chess.com, ChessTempo, CT-ART, Chessable tactics, Aimchess), and the best CALCULATION-training books (e.g. works on calculation/visualization/candidate moves). Distinguish pattern-recognition tools from deep-calculation training. Prioritize what moves a 2000+ tactician toward 2300+.

METHOD: Use WebSearch to find candidate resources, then WebFetch the most promising official pages (Chessable, publisher sites, YouTube, Amazon, chess.com, lichess) to confirm titles, authors, prices, and URLs. Do MULTIPLE searches with varied queries. Return 6-12 REAL, currently-available resources. Every "why" MUST reference a specific item from Scrodinger's profile (a named weakness, his repertoire, his strength, or his rating goal) — no generic justifications. Be concrete with prices and canonical URLs. Mark each Essential/Recommended/Optional.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
Web search results for query: "ChessTempo vs CT-ART vs Chessable tactics trainer deep calculation"

Links: [{"title":"Improving Your Chess: Cost and Quality | US Chess.org","url":"https://new.uschess.org/news/improving-chess-cost-quality"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found comparing these tactics training platforms:

**ChessTempo vs CT-ART vs Chessable:**

ChessTempo.com offers a Diamond Membership for $79 per year and has cool features that many players consider great for tactical training, though some find the interface less appealing and the problem solutions too computerized.

CT-ART is a program available through ChessKing Apps (costing about $8 per app) and is known as their flagship software for chess tactics training.

Chessable.com is best for purchasing standalone courses, especially opening repertoires, as the spaced repetition training is hard to beat.

The key distinction is that these platforms serve somewhat different purposes: ChessTempo and CT-ART are more focused on tactical puzzles and problem-solving, while Chessable excels at structured course-based learning with spaced repetition. The choice depends on your learning style—whether you prefer intensive puzzle training or structured courses with repetition intervals.

Links: [{"title":"Book recommendations for calculation training • page 2/2 • General Chess Discussion • lichess.org","url":"https://lichess.org/forum/general-chess-discussion/book-recommendations-for-calculation-training?page=2"},{"title":"What are the best books for improving calculation skills as opposed to spotting tactics? - Chess Forums - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/forum/view/general/what-are-the-best-books-for-improving-calculation-skills-as-opposed-to-spotting-tactics"},{"title":"Cognitive Chess: Improving Visualization and Calculation Skills: Chernyshov, Konstantin, Harris, Ian: 9781949859447: Amazon.com: Books","url":"https://www.amazon.com/Cognitive-Chess-Improving-Visualization-Calculation/dp/1949859444"},{"title":"Improve Your Chess Calculation - New In Chess","url":"https://www.newinchess.com/improve-your-chess-calculation"},{"title":"Book on Calculation - Chess Forums - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/forum/view/chess-equipment/book-on-calculation"},{"title":"Improve Your Chess Calculation: The Ramesh Chess Course: RB, Ramesh: 9789056919979: Amazon.com: Books","url":"https://www.amazon.com/Improve-Your-Chess-Calculation-Ramesh/dp/9056919970"},{"title":"9 Best Books to Improve Chess Calculation - chessarticle.com","url":"https://chessarticle.com/best-books-to-improve-chess-calculation/"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best chess calculation training books recommended for 2000+ level players:

## Top Recommendations for 2000+ Players

**Grandmaster Preparation: Calculation by Jacob Aagaard**
This book is recommended for players at the 2000 rapid level. The book is very tough and you should expect to spend hours on a single puzzle. The key approach is not to solve puzzles like a tactics book, but to set up the position, set a timer for 20 minutes, and try to calculate the winning variation all the way through without guessing.

**Improve Your Chess Calculation by Grandmaster Ramesh RB**
You set up positions on a chessboard and solve exercises without moving the pieces, with GM Ramesh providing carefully selected exercises. This is designed as visualization training exercises for advanced chess players to learn to visualize and calculate 10 to 16 moves ahead.

**Cognitive Chess by Konstantin Chernyshov**
Cognitive Chess is designed to train you to visualize the board and correctly calculate sequences in your mind, skills that are essential to problem solving in all phases of the game.

**Finding Chess Jewels by Michal Krasenkow**
Finding Chess Jewels is recommended for 2000+ chess players.

**Other Notable Books:**
The Inner Game of Chess by Soltis is an excellent resource for calculation skills and goes well beyond Kotov with practical approaches to calculating. Dvoretsky's Endgame Manual is recommended, as endgames are a great way to learn better calculation and visualization.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Aimchess in 2026:

## Overview

Aimchess is an AI-powered chess analytics and training platform that creates personalized puzzles and lessons based on analysis of your own games from Chess.com, Lichess, or Chess24. The platform was acquired by Play Magnus Group in 2026, which was then acquired by Chess.com in 2026, meaning Aimchess now operates under the same umbrella as Chess.com itself.

## Key Features

When you sign up for Aimchess, the system analyzes every move from your recent games and measures your skill set across six core aspects of chess, then compares you to others with the same rating to show you what you need to improve on. These six dimensions are: opening accuracy, advantage capitalization, time management, resourcefulness, endgame, and blunder prevention.

## Pricing

Aimchess pricing is around $15/month. However, there's a plan that goes for just $7.99 monthly with unlimited analysis reports on your games. The meaningful features including personalized weakness analysis, daily drills, and full opening performance breakdown are locked behind a subscription that runs around $12-15 per month as of early 2026.

## Assessment

Aimchess is the most sophisticated dedicated AI analysis platform for chess improvement in 2026 — it analyzes patterns across hundreds of your games and identifies specific recurring weaknesses rather than just evaluating individual moves. Aimchess is worth the subscription if you're playing 20+ games per week and actively working on improvement — roughly at the 1400+ level.

A Complete Guide for Chess Players | Peck","url":"https://peckchess.com/blog/what-is-the-woodpecker-method"},{"title":"Does the Woodpecker Method actually work? Results From 3,000 Chess Tactics in 17 Days","url":"https://medium.com/@hello_89167/the-woodpecker-method-in-practice-3-000-chess-tactics-in-17-days-bc257eac8a99"},{"title":"The Woodpecker Method - Axel Smith & Hans Tikkanen","url":"https://forwardchess.com/product/the-woodpecker-method"},{"title":"The Woodpecker Method Explained: Turbocharge Your Tactical and Strategic Vision - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/Chessable/the-woodpecker-method-explained-turbocharge-your-tactical-and-strategic-vision"},{"title":"The Woodpecker Method: A Complete Guide | ChessWoodie","url":"https://www.chesswoodie.com/blog/woodpecker-method/"},{"title":"My First Experience with The Woodpecker Method - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/thrillchester/my-first-experience-with-the-woodpecker-method"}]

Here are the search results for the Woodpecker Method:

## Overview

The Woodpecker Method is based on a book by Axel Smith and Hans Tikkanen, offering a unique approach to chess training that improves calculation and pattern recognition skills.

## Core Concept

The method entails repetitive solving of the same set of chess problems until patterns are deeply ingrained in the player's memory, similar to how a woodpecker repetitively strikes the same spot. Unlike traditional puzzle training where you solve new puzzles every day, the Woodpecker Method focuses on repetition and speed, where you select a fixed set of tactical puzzles and solve them in cycles, getting faster each time.

## Pattern Recognition and Speed

Regular exposure to the same set of problems reinforces patterns and tactical motifs in a player's mind, and over time, these patterns become second nature, allowing for quicker recognition during actual games. Most players see a 4–8× improvement in cycle time by their 4th or 5th cycle. If your first cycle takes 60 minutes, you might finish cycle 5 in just 8–10 minutes.

## Focus on Quality

Instead of jumping from one random puzzle to the next, players focus on a select set, ensuring quality over quantity in their training.

## Notable Results

Hans Tikkanen trained with the method in the Spring of 2010 and achieved three GM norms as well as +2500 rating that Summer.

Humor & Entertainment Kindle eBooks @ Amazon.com.","url":"https://www.amazon.com/Improve-your-Chess-Calculation-Ramesh-ebook/dp/B0B4PWL933"},{"title":"GM Ramesh’s \"Improve Your Chess Calculation\": A Complete Review","url":"https://www.attackingchess.com/gm-rameshs-improve-your-chess-calculation-a-complete-review/"},{"title":"Improve Your Chess Calculation: The Ramesh Chess Course - Ramesh RB - Google Books","url":"https://books.google.com/books/about/Improve_Your_Chess_Calculation.html?id=LBy5zgEACAAJ"},{"title":"Chess Calculation Insights From GM RB Ramesh","url":"https://saychess.substack.com/p/chess-calculation-insights-from-gm"},{"title":"Improve Your Chess Calculation: The Ramesh Chess Course by Ramesh RB | Goodreads","url":"https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/61419956-improve-your-chess-calculation"},{"title":"Improve Your Chess Calculation: The Ramesh Chess Course - Volume 1 - English Chess Forum","url":"https://www.ecforum.org.uk/viewtopic.php?t=13152"},{"title":"Ramesh RB Improve Your Chess Calculation The Ramesh Chess Course, Volume 1","url":"https://www.newinchess.com/media/wysiwyg/product_pdf/9138.pdf"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of "Improve Your Chess Calculation" by GM Ramesh RB, published by New In Chess:

## Book Overview

Improve Your Chess Calculation is a best selling chess book and chessable course, designed to help students enhance that allusive skill. The book won the 2022 Chess.com Book of the Year Award.

## Author's Credentials

GM Ramesh RB is a former British Champion and world-renowned coaching guru who has helped luminaries like Praggnanandhaa and Daniel Naroditsky to improve at the highest levels. GM Ramesh is one of the world's most successful coaches who has trained many of India's top talents at all stages of their development on their journey to become International Masters and Grandmasters.

## Positive Reviews

Reviewers give it two thumbs up and recommend it to any player who is serious about improving their ability to calculate complex positions. GM Daniel Naroditsky states: "After only a month of intensive training with Ramesh, I could sense a seismic shift in both the precision of my calculation as well as my general level of sharpness."

The book is considered one of the most important chess training books of the modern era.

## Content and Approach

The book is not a puzzle book in the traditional sense but a training manual structured to simulate the intensity of a coaching session at Ramesh's "Chess Gurukul" in Chennai. Ramesh categorizes the material into five levels based on Elo rating, ranging from Level 1 (1200–1600) to Level 5 (2600 and above).

## Criticisms

The content of the book is very stretching and requires a very disciplined approach to the resolution of its training material, such as sitting with an actual chess board and pieces and formulating solutions without moving the pieces. Some readers found the explanations insufficient or the exercises too challenging for their level.

# The Woodpecker Method

**Title:** The Woodpecker Method

**Authors:** Axel Smith & Hans Tikkanen

**Publisher:** Quality Chess

**Price:** $23.99 USD

**What It Trains:** This book focuses on tactical chess improvement through "over 1,100 tactical puzzles" arranged progressively from beginner to advanced levels. The method emphasizes developing calculation skills, pattern recognition, decision-making speed, board vision, and confidence through structured, repetitive practice designed to "re-program" unconscious chess instincts.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about these two chess books and their approaches to calculation and visualization:

## Tisdall's "Improve Your Chess Now"

Tisdall's book includes exercises to develop calculation skills, including the use of 'stepping-stone diagrams' (a Tisdall original - while trying to visualise a position some moves hence, a player fixes a half-way position in the mind's eye, then, when this is secure, continues calculating).

## Soltis's "The Inner Game of Chess"

In The Inner Game of Chess, GM Andy Soltis shows that the key to good calculation is good visualization of the position in front of us and then after the moves we're considering. This book examines both the technical and practical aspects of how to think ahead -- the selection of candidate moves, the evaluation of end positions, finding the proper move order, and the like, with special attention paid to the broad range of calculating mistakes, such as overlooked zwischenzugs and "quiet moves," visualization blunders, and "believing" your opponent.

## Comparison of Approaches

The two books take somewhat different approaches. Soltis doesn't introduce any methods, stepping stones, etc., nor provides any kind of systematic treatment of the subject. In contrast, Tisdall's book supplements the concepts put forth by Soltis and both approaches meld nicely, with both authors being critical of Kotov's tree scheme and offering sensible, practical alternatives.

content.text: I now have a complete, verified set of resources spanning pattern-recognition/speed tools and deep-calculation training. Let me compile the structured output, tying each to Scrodinger's specific profile points.

resources.name: Grandmaster Preparation: Calculation (Revised & Expanded)
resources.why: This is THE deep-calculation book for a player at exactly Scrodinger's level. It is organized by thinking method (Candidates, Comparison, Elimination, Intermediate Moves, Imagination, Prophylaxis) rather than by tactical motif, which directly attacks his #4 weakness (losing the thread in the middlegame transition) and his blitz-vs-rapid gap (#3) by making candidate-move generation a disciplined habit instead of an intuitive reflex. A 2073-2212 tactician who already SEES tactics needs depth, and Aagaard's 20-minutes-per-position regimen builds exactly that.

resources.name: Improve Your Chess Calculation: The Ramesh Chess Course
resources.provider: GM R.B. Ramesh / New In Chess
resources.why: Chess.com 2022 Book of the Year, written by Naroditsky's and Praggnanandhaa's coach. It is built around solving WITHOUT moving the pieces, which trains the visualization Scrodinger needs to make calculation automatic under blitz time pressure (his #3 weakness, blitz 500+ pts below rapid). The leveled structure (Level 4-5 target him) and explicit work on correcting thought-process mistakes also addresses his middlegame-transition leak (#4). Pairs naturally with the Chessable version for spaced repetition.

resources.name: The Woodpecker Method
resources.provider: Axel Smith & Hans Tikkanen / Quality Chess
resources.why: Directly targets Scrodinger's #3 weakness: his blitz lags rapid by 500 points because pattern recognition is not yet automatic at speed. Woodpecker's repetition cycles (solve the same 1,100+ puzzles faster each pass) convert known patterns into instant reflexes - Tikkanen gained 3 GM norms and +2500 with it. Use the Advanced set to push his fast recognition toward the level his calculation already supports. This is a pattern/SPEED tool, complementary to the calculation-DEPTH books above.

resources.name: Improve Your Chess Calculation (Chessable MoveTrainer course)
resources.cost: Paid course (one-time); free sample lesson available
resources.why: Same elite Ramesh material as the book but delivered with video plus Chessable's spaced repetition, which is ideal for Scrodinger as an online-platform player (chess.com/lichess). Spaced repetition forces the calculation habits to stick between sessions so they survive into blitz - again hitting his #3 gap. Choosing the digital version over (or alongside) paper suits a fast self-improver who already trains heavily online.

resources.name: ChessTempo Diamond Membership (Tactics + Standard/Blitz modes)
resources.why: ChessTempo's rated Standard tactics (untimed, hard) build calculation depth while its Blitz tactics mode trains the speed Scrodinger lacks (#3) - letting him work BOTH dimensions on one platform. Crucially, its custom problem sets let him filter by motif and by phase, so he can drill the coordinated-attack king-safety patterns he keeps losing to (#2 weakness). The deep stats also reveal which tactical themes are slow for him - data his current chess.com/lichess puzzle ratings (2073-2212) don't break down.

resources.name: The Inner Game of Chess: How to Calculate and Win
resources.why: A readable counterpart to Aagaard that explicitly catalogues calculation ERRORS - overlooked zwischenzugs, quiet moves, visualization blunders, move-order mistakes. For a strong tactician (2073+) whose problem is not seeing tactics but losing the thread in conversion (#4) and missing depth under pressure (#3), naming his own error types is high-leverage. Lower friction than the Grandmaster Preparation grind, good to read in parallel.

resources.name: Improve Your Chess Now (New Edition)
resources.why: Introduces 'stepping-stone diagrams' - fixing a half-way position in the mind's eye before calculating further - a concrete visualization technique that directly serves Scrodinger's need to calculate deeper and more reliably at speed (#3). This trainable method complements Soltis's error-catalogue and Ramesh's no-move-the-pieces drills, giving him an actual mental scaffold rather than just more puzzles.

resources.name: CT-ART 4.0 / 6.0 (Chess Tactics Art)
resources.why: The classic graded tactics engine - thousands of puzzles rising to ~2400, with hints and themed drilling. Cheap, structured pattern-recognition volume to feed Woodpecker-style speed work and reinforce attacking/king-safety motifs (#2). Useful as a mobile companion for a player who improves fast and trains daily, but it is pure pattern volume - not deep-calculation training, so it ranks below the Aagaard/Ramesh core.

resources.name: Aimchess
resources.why: Analyzes Scrodinger's own chess.com/lichess games and generates puzzles from HIS recurring blunder patterns and time-management leaks - directly surfacing the king-safety collapses (#2) and the blitz speed/blunder problem (#3) rather than feeding generic puzzles. For a player whose data already shows a specific profile (Black 32% vs White 48% win rate), personalized weakness drilling is more efficient than untargeted tactics. Optional because the book/ChessTempo core matters more for raw depth.

content.text:
Research complete. I returned 9 verified, currently-available Tactics & Calculation Training resources for Scrodinger, deliberately split between the two skills his profile separates:

**Deep-calculation DEPTH (his real gap — he already sees tactics at 2073-2212):**
- Aagaard, *Grandmaster Preparation: Calculation* (Essential) — method-based candidate-move training, the 2000→2300+ standard
- Ramesh, *Improve Your Chess Calculation* book + Chessable course (Essential/Recommended) — no-move-the-pieces visualization
- Soltis, *Inner Game of Chess* and Tisdall, *Improve Your Chess Now* — calculation error-catalogue + stepping-stone visualization technique

**Pattern-recognition SPEED (his blitz-vs-rapid 500-pt gap):**
- Smith/Tikkanen, *The Woodpecker Method* (Essential) — repetition cycles that automate patterns at speed
- ChessTempo Diamond (Recommended) — both untimed Standard and Blitz tactics on one platform, phase/motif filtering
- CT-ART and Aimchess (Optional) — cheap graded volume; Aimchess drills his own game-data weaknesses

Every "why" ties to a named profile item: his #3 blitz-lag, #2 king-safety/coordinated-attack losses, #4 middlegame-transition leak, his 2073-2212 tactical strength, or his 2300+ trajectory. Prices and canonical URLs were confirmed via WebFetch on Quality Chess/New In Chess/Forward Chess/Chessable official pages where possible. The full structured list has been submitted via StructuredOutput.
